about.text: My name is Jelle Schrijvers and I am a student in the Associate Degree Programming at Hogeschool PXL. This online portfolio was created as part of the Workplace Learning 1 course and serves as a reflection of my learning process throughout this trajectory. In this portfolio, I document my experiences, assignments, and projects that I have carried out both at school and in the workplace. The focus here is on developing my technical skills, my problem-solving thinking, and my professional attitude. Additionally, this portfolio contains reflections on my growth as a beginning programmer and the feedback I received from my lecturers. Each section is structured to provide a clear and organized overview of my progress. The portfolio is intended as a tool for evaluation, guidance, and self-reflection. In this way, it forms not only an overview of my achieved results but also of the learning objectives I have actively worked on.

opleiding.title: Choice of Study
opleiding.why_programming_p1: My interest in programming started at a young age, during talent labs in my first year of secondary school. That was where I first encountered logical thinking and problem-solving through small programming assignments. Solving those assignments gave me an immediate sense of satisfaction.
opleiding.why_programming_p2: In the following years this interest deepened through STEM science courses. Projects with Minecraft Education, robotics, and automation strengthened my enthusiasm for combining creativity with technology.
opleiding.why_programming_p3: What appeals to me most about programming is solving problems and building creative solutions. Programming is both a passion and a future-oriented career choice for me.
opleiding.why_job_title: Why does this profession suit me?
opleiding.why_job_p1: The profession of developer fits well with the way I work. I enjoy working independently, experimenting with solutions, and learning from mistakes.
opleiding.why_job_p2: When a problem becomes complex, I consciously step back so I can return to it with a fresh perspective. This helps me to persist even with challenging assignments.
opleiding.why_job_p3: In addition, I possess qualities such as problem-solving thinking, creativity, and perseverance — all of which are essential in this profession.

reflectie.title: 5.1 AIM Reflection on WPL1
reflectie.introductie: My work-based learning period (WPL1) had its ups and downs, but I learned a great deal about myself and my skills.
reflectie.positief: Through practical assignments I made noticeable progress in programming, especially in C#.
reflectie.minder: The time pressure during practical exams was challenging, but gave me a realistic picture of the professional field.
reflectie.inzichten: I work well independently and in a structured way, but I can still grow in managing stress and deadlines.
reflectie.succes: My greatest success is that I have come to know myself better and know where I can improve.

volgendewpl.title: 5.2 What I take into the next WPL
volgendewpl.p1: My independence, creativity, and boldness are strong points I want to keep using. At the same time I want to work more consciously on collaboration and communication.
volgendewpl.p2: Concretely, I want to ask for feedback more often, consult with others, and share my ideas better within a team context.

leiderschap.title: Leadership / Initiative
leiderschap.tekst: I take responsibility for my own learning process and start assignments proactively.

creativiteit.title: Creativity / Innovation
creativiteit.tekst: I always work out a design first before I start programming.

samenwerking.title: Collaboration / Communication
samenwerking.tekst: I actively ask for feedback from lecturers and classmates.

professioneel.title: Professional Development
professioneel.tekst: I keep learning and applying new techniques in my projects.

labels.goed: What are you already good at?
labels.niet: What are you not yet good at?

ontwerper.title: Designer
ontwerper.situatie: I can create my own design and reproduce it within a few days. Given enough time I can work out many ideas well.
ontwerper.goed: Building a basic idea for a project works well. I can work out an initial structure or concept.
ontwerper.niet: It is harder to produce a fully worked-out project in a short time, including the right functions and choice of programming languages.

programmeur.title: Programmer
programmeur.situatie: I can build projects independently and usually work on my assignments without problems at a relaxed pace.
programmeur.goed: I am strong at building websites. In C# almost everything works, except working with classes.
programmeur.niet: Correctly using classes in C# remains a challenge. I can create them, but applying them in projects is still difficult.

tester.title: Tester
tester.situatie: I can find and fix errors fairly well, but I am less good at spotting typos.
tester.goed: I am good at debugging both practical and logical errors in code.
tester.niet: Recognising typos in code is something I am not yet so good at.

communicator.title: Communicator / Team Player
communicator.situatie: I find working in a team difficult, but I am good at asking for feedback.
communicator.goed: I can ask for feedback, process it, and improve my work based on it.
communicator.niet: I prefer working independently and find it difficult to constantly consult within a team.

levenslang.title: Lifelong Learning IT Professional
levenslang.situatie: I follow new technologies to a limited extent, but I am very strict with myself about personal development.
levenslang.goed: I continually try to improve myself and work as independently as possible without constant external help.
levenslang.niet: I sometimes go too far by constantly adding new features, causing other important parts to be neglected.

items.title: Designing my CV website
items.beschrijving: For my CV I first created a design with layout, structure, colours, and navigation before I started programming.
items.motivatie: This activity belongs to the Designer role because it prepares the realisation of a software project.

items.title: Choice of programming languages
items.beschrijving: For web projects I deliberately chose HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
items.motivatie: Justifying language choices is part of the design process.

items.title: Determining functionalities
items.beschrijving: I decided in advance which functionalities my website needed to contain.
items.motivatie: Planning functionalities is a design task.

items.title: Building websites
items.beschrijving: I developed websites with HTML and CSS, including responsive design.
items.motivatie: This is the effective realisation of software.

items.title: C# console applications
items.beschrijving: I worked on C# assignments involving logic, methods, and variables.
items.motivatie: This is at the core of programming.

items.title: Using GitHub
items.beschrijving: I used GitHub to manage my projects.
items.motivatie: This demonstrates responsibility for the work environment.

items.title: Responsive testing
items.beschrijving: I tested websites on desktop, tablet, and mobile.
items.motivatie: This is testing functionality and layout.

items.title: Debugging errors
items.beschrijving: I analysed and fixed logical errors in code.

items.title: Re-testing after feedback
items.beschrijving: I retested my code after making changes.
items.motivatie: Improving based on feedback is part of testing.

items.title: Asking for feedback
items.beschrijving: I asked lecturers for feedback on my projects.

items.title: Processing feedback
items.beschrijving: I adjusted my projects based on feedback received.

items.title: Explaining my work
items.beschrijving: I explained my code to others.
items.motivatie: Clarifying your work is part of communication.

items.title: Learning new techniques
items.beschrijving: I independently learned new CSS and JavaScript techniques.

items.title: Future improvement
items.beschrijving: I want to learn to plan better in order to maintain focus.
items.motivatie: This demonstrates awareness of areas for growth.

inhoud: POP motivation, core quadrants and reflection
